task: I’m putting together a small-form-factor AM5 build and I want to base it on the ASUS ROG Strix B650E-I Gaming WiFi, so please start on the official ASUS site and open the exact product page for that motherboard, then leave it open in its own tab so I can glance at the specs and photos later. After that, use Google to find a community-made AM5 motherboard tier list from a forum, spreadsheet, Reddit post, or similar enthusiast source, open the actual tier list page, and check where the ASUS ROG Strix B650E-I Gaming WiFi shows up so I can sanity-check whether this is still considered a solid pick. If the board looks reasonable there, go to Amazon and find the product page for the Thermalright Peerless Assassin CPU cooler as a possible pairing for the build, and leave that open too so I can compare it visually with the motherboard tab. In the end, send me the three direct links and a short note saying what tier or listing the ASUS board got and whether that makes it seem like a sensible choice for this build.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official ASUS product page for the ROG Strix B650E-I Gaming WiFi. The URL provided in the final response (https://rog.asus.com/motherboards/rog-strix/rog-strix-b650e-i-gaming-wifi-model/) is correct, and the page title matches the requirement.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for and identified a community-created AM5 motherboard spreadsheet (a common enthusiast resource for this purpose). It opened the spreadsheet in its own tab (tab 4 in the history, later confirmed as the source of the data). The agent verified the board's presence in the spreadsheet and provided the direct URL to the sheet in its final response. This satisfies the requirement of providing a direct URL to a community-created AM5 motherboard tier list/comparison source.

R3: success - The agent successfully identified a community-made AM5 motherboard spreadsheet (the "AM5 Motherboards Sheet"). It navigated to the B650(E) tab, extracted the specific row for the "ROG STRIX B650E-I WIFI", and correctly noted that the spreadsheet is a detailed comparison list rather than a ranked tier list. In its final note, the agent accurately described the board's placement in the sheet (B650(E) tab) and provided a reasonable assessment of why it is a solid pick based on the specs found (m-ITX, B650E, PCIe 5.0, etc.). This satisfies the requirement to report where the board appears and provide a sanity-check note.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to Amazon, searched for the "Thermalright Peerless Assassin CPU cooler," and opened the product page. The final response includes the direct URL `https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09LGY38L4`, which corresponds to the Thermalright Peerless Assassin 120 SE CPU cooler as verified in the agent's history (Step 30/32).
